segments.s658-g177: The numeral 'دو' (two) precedes the noun phrase and enumerates items. 'یاره' here names a small piece of jewelry (the precise semantic nuance is archaic/poetic) and is modified by 'بهاگیر' as a postpositive qualifier meaning costly/valuable; 'و' coordinates the next noun phrase 'دو گوشوار' (two earrings). The whole segment lists two distinct pairs of ornaments.
segments.s658-g178: 'یکی' is the numeral 'one' (here introducing a single item). 'طوق' is the head noun 'necklace/collar'; 'پرگوهر' is a compound adjective ('پر-' = full of) meaning 'full of gems / jewelled'; 'شاهوار' is an adjectival epithet 'kingly/regal' modifying the necklace. Word order is determiner–noun–adjectives, typical in Persian poetry.
segments.s658-g179: 'هم' here is an adverb 'also/too'. 'از' is the partitive/prepositional 'from/of'. 'شاره هندی' is a two‑word phrase (shāreh + 'هندی' as postmodifier) referring to an Indian material or named object (an archaic term); 'و' links it to 'تیغ هند' ('sword of India' / 'Indian sword'), where 'تیغ' = 'sword' and 'هند' functions adjectivally as 'Indian'. The segment enumerates types/materials.
segments.s658-g180: 'همه' is the subject 'all' or 'every one'. 'روی' here means 'face' or 'surface' (of the objects just named). 'آهن' names the material 'iron'; 'سراسر' is an adverb/adjectival modifier meaning 'throughout' or 'entirely'. 'پرند' is an archaic/poetic verb‑form meaning 'are filled/covered' (i.e., 'were entirely of iron'); syntactically it gives the predicate agreeing with 'همه'.
segments.s658-g181: 'بیامد' is the simple past 'came'. 'ز' (poetic variant of 'از') marks origin 'from', here 'قنوج' (Kannauj). 'برزوی' is the proper name (Barzū) placed after the prepositional phrase by poetic inversion; 'شاد' is an adjective 'happy/joyful' describing him. The clause therefore states who came and from where, with the name and epithet following the verb.
segments.s658-g182: 'بسی' functions as an intensifier 'much/a great deal' (classical usage). 'دانش' is 'knowledge/learning' and 'نو' modifies it as 'new'. 'گرفته' is a past participle 'taken/obtained' used adjectivally ('having acquired'); 'به یاد' is a prepositional phrase 'to memory' / 'in mind', indicating retention. The sequence describes that he had acquired much new knowledge (which he carried in memory).
segments.s658-g183: 'ز ره' = 'from the road' (z = poetic 'from', 'ره' = way/road). 'چون' is used as a temporal conjunction 'when/as', and 'رسید' is the past 'arrived/reached'. 'اندر' = 'into/in', 'آن بارگاه' = 'that palace/court'. Word order places the temporal clause before the rest of the narrative, a common epic device.
segments.s658-g184: 'نیایش کنان' is an adverbial participial phrase: 'نیایش' = 'prayer/supplication' and 'کنان' is the verbal‑adverbial form 'doing' (so 'praying'). 'رفت' is the past 'went', and 'نزدیک شاه' is the directional/locative phrase 'near the king'. Together they say that, while praying, he went up close to the king.
segments.s658-g113: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. نزدیک او شد literally 'became near to him' (شد + نزدیک) functions as 'approached him'. سخنگوی مرد is a noun phrase 'the man's spokesman' (speaker/representative) that follows the temporal clause as the subject of what happens next.
segments.s658-g114: همه رنج‌ها is the object 'all the sufferings/hardships'. پیش او is a locative/adverbial phrase 'before him / in his presence'. یاد کرد is a compound verbal idea: یاد (remembrance/mention) + کرد (did) = 'recounted / mentioned' in simple past.
segments.s658-g115: ز کار نبشته is a genitival/ablative phrase 'from the written account of the deed/affair' (ز = 'from', کار = 'deed/affair', نبشته = 'written record'). که آمد پدید is a relative/result clause 'which came to light' (آمد پدید = 'appeared').
segments.s658-g116: سخن‌ها 'the words/sayings' is followed by a relative clause که از کاردانان شنید 'which he heard from the experts' — از marks the source ('from'), کاردانان = 'skilled/knowledgeable people', شنید is simple past 'heard' (here describing what those words were).
segments.s658-g117: بدو = 'to him' (directional dative). پیر دانا is a two-word epithet 'the old, wise (man)'. زبان برگشاد is a figurative idiom 'opened (his) tongue' = 'began to speak'; برگشاد is the past participle 'opened'.
segments.s658-g118: ز هر دانشی 'out of every kind of knowledge' (ز = 'out of/from', هر دانشی = 'each/any knowledge'). پیش او 'before him / in his presence'. کرد یاد is again 'made mention / recounted' (کرد + یاد), with یاد the noun 'remembrance' used as a verbal object.
segments.s658-g119: که introduces reported content 'that'. من در نبشته چنین یافتم = 'I found/learned thus in the writing/record' — در نبشته is locative 'in the written text', چنین 'thus', یافتم simple past 'I found'.
segments.s658-g120: بدان آرزو is a directional phrase 'to/ toward that desire' (ب as directional prefix + دان/دانِ = 'that' in older usage, here functioning as a demonstrative). تیز is an adverbial modifier 'swift(ly)', and بشتافتم is first-person past 'I hurried / I hastened'.
segments.s658-g265: نگه کن is an imperative phrase: نگه (look/gaze) + کن (do! — the imperative of کردن), so together it functions like English “behold” or “look.” کنون is an adverb “now/presently” placed immediately after the imperative for temporal emphasis. کار بوزرجمهر is a noun phrase: کار (deed/affair) with the proper name بوزرجمهر following it as its possessor — “the deed of Bozorgmehr.”
segments.s658-g266: که introduces a relative clause (“which/that”) modifying the preceding noun (کار). از خاک is a simple prepositional phrase “from the earth/dust.” بر شد combines the prefix/particle بر (“up/on”) with the past verb شد (here used as a lexical past “rose/was raised”), so together “rose up.” به گردان سپهر is a directional phrase: به “to/toward” + گردان as an adjectival/attributive form “circling/revolving” modifying سپهر “heaven/sky.”
segments.s658-g267: فراز آوریدش is an imperative/command construction: فراز “on high/above” + آورید‌ش (آورید + ضمیر شِ — “bring him”), literally “bring/raise him aloft.” به خاک نژند completes the direction: به “to/over” + خاک “earth” + نژند “base/worthless,” so together “above the lowly/base earth.” Word order places the locative phrase after the verb, as is normal in Persian commands.
segments.s658-g268: همان کس که is a resumptive noun phrase introducing a relative clause: همان کس “the same person” + که “who/that.” بردش here is the past verb بردن with the object pronoun ـش (“him”), meaning “carried/took him” (the gloss list’s senses are imperfectly aligned but the function is past transitive). به ابر بلند is a directional/locative phrase: به “to” + ابر “cloud” + بلند “high/lofty,” i.e. “to the high cloud.”
segments.s658-g193: This clause lists possessions: subject is implicit (he) and the main verb بود is past tense 'was/there was' used existentially. The coordination 'درم بود و گوهر چپ و دست راست' strings together two noun phrases (درم, گوهر) and adds a locative description 'چپ و دست راست' after گوهر to indicate 'in the left and right hand'. Word order is typical Persian: theme (possessions) before the copula/verb.
segments.s658-g194: جز ... نخواست is a negative-except construction: جز 'except' + از + object 'جامه شاه' and the main verb نخواست (past of خواستن) 'he did not want/asked for nothing'. The particle از marks source/possession ('from the king'), and چیزی is an indefinite object placed before the verb and negated by the construction (here rendered as 'wanted nothing except...').
segments.s658-g195: گرانمایه modifies دستی ('a costly/precious hand-ornament'); بپوشید is past tense (3rd singular) of پوشیدن 'to put on/don'. The conjunction و links this clause to the following verb رفت 'he went/left'. The phrase is compact: adjective + noun + verb, standard Persian order.
segments.s658-g196: بر گاه کسری is a prepositional phrase meaning 'to/into the court/presence of Kisra (Khosrow)' with بر + گاه (here 'place/time' = 'court, presence'). خرامید is past tense 'he strode/pranced proudly'; تفت stands as a short poetic tag modifying the manner (here intensifying the proud stride). Word order places the place phrase before the verb, a normal Persian arrangement for emphasis.
segments.s658-g197: چو آمد is an archaic/poetic 'when he came' (چو = 'when'); به نزدیک تختش 'to near his throne' is the locative complement (به + نزدیک + تختش). فراز here functions adverbially 'up/on high' and indicates elevation (literally 'on high', i.e. to a higher place beside the throne). The clause sets the temporal-location scene for the following actions.
segments.s658-g198: This sequence records three short actions. برو is an imperative/exhortation 'go/rise' used here as a bid to ascend or approach; آفرین کرد is a light-verb construction meaning 'made acclamation/praised' (آفرین = acclamation; کرد = did/made); و links to بردش نماز where بردش (برد + pronoun) 'took/carried him' (led him) and نماز is the goal 'to prayer' — together 'they bade him rise, praised him, and took him to the prayer'. Verb forms are past or perfective narrative; word order places the verb phrase after the brief imperative and noun.
segments.s658-g199: بدو گفت is 'he said to him' (بدو = 'to him', گفت = 'said'); پس 'then/afterwards' is a temporal adverb; نامور شهریار is the vocative or appositive 'the famed sovereign/king'. The clause is a reporting clause that introduces direct speech or a following description.
segments.s658-g200: This fragment begins the content of what was said: که introduces a clause 'that/which'; بی + noun = 'without X'; بدره here is a poetic term often meaning 'full moon' used metaphorically for beauty; و گوهر 'and jewel'; شاهوار is an adjective 'kingly/regal' modifying the following implied noun. The line is elliptical/poetic and leaves the full sense implicit.
segments.s658-g241: This is a verbless nominal clause typical of poetic diction: 'گرانمایه بوالفضل' is a descriptive noun phrase 'the esteemed Abu al‑Fazl' and 'دستور اوی' functions as a predicate (dastur as an office/title). اوی is a possessive here ('his'), so دستور اوی = 'his dastur' (i.e. his chief official/adviser); the linking copula 'was' is omitted in the line.
segments.s658-g242: Relative clause introduced by que/که 'that/who': 'اندر سخن بود' = 'in speech he was' (بود = past 'was'), and 'گنجور اوی' = 'a treasure‑house belonging to him' (گنجور used metaphorically = 'treasure‑house' of words/phrases). The clause qualifies the previous noun (Abu al‑Fazl) — 'who in speech was a treasure‑house.'
segments.s658-g243: بفرمود is an honorific/polite past 3rd‑person verb 'he ordered/said'. تا introduces the subordinate/purpose clause 'that ...', so پارسی و دری are the languages specified as the medium to be used in the following verb clause.
segments.s658-g244: نبشتند is past plural 'they wrote' (the action ordered). و links to the result/clause 'کوتاه شد داوری' — کوتاه شد is a passive/resultative phrase 'was shortened' or 'became short', meaning 'the adjudication/decision (داوری) was brief/was cut short.'
segments.s658-g245: وزان پس 'and from that, then' is a temporal transition. چو + پیوسته رای آمدش = 'when continuous/steady counsel came to him' — چو 'when', پیوسته 'continuous/steady' modifies رای 'counsel/plan', and آمدش is the past 'came to him.'
segments.s658-g246: بدانش here functions as a dative/locative 'to that / thereupon / to him' linking the previous clause; 'خرد رهنمای' is a noun phrase 'a wise guide' (خرد 'wisdom' used adjectivally), and آمدش again 'came to him' — literal: 'to that a wise guide came to him.'
segments.s658-g247: همی‌خواست is an imperfective/habitual past 'he wished/desired' (the prefix همی gives the continuous/characteristic poetic aspect). تا introduces purpose/result: 'that openly and secretly' (آشکار و نهان = 'in public and in private').
segments.s658-g248: ازو 'of him / from him' marks possession/source: 'a یادگاری (memorial/inscription/memento) of him' بود is the past 'was/should be' and در جهان 'in the world' gives the location/scope — together: 'a memorial of him should exist in the world.'
segments.s658-g217: بدان is the imperative formed from ب + دان (prefix be- + root 'to know') meaning 'know' or 'be aware'. تا introduces the following clause with a purposive/quotative sense ('so that' / 'that'); پس از = 'after' (پس + از) and در جهان = 'in the world'. Word order is slightly inverted for poetic effect but functions as 'Know that after my death in the world...'.
segments.s658-g218: ز here means 'from' (poetic/prepositional). داننده = 'the knower' or someone who understands; رنجم = 'my suffering' (رنج + enclitic ـم). نگردد is an archaic negative of گشت/گردیدن meaning 'become' or 'be made' (here 'become / be left'), so نگردد نهان = 'is not made hidden' / 'is not hidden'. The whole segment states that the speaker's sorrow should not remain hidden from the knower.
segments.s658-g219: بدو گفت شاه is inverted order for شاه به او گفت — 'the king said to him'. بدو = به + او 'to him'. این بزرگ آرزوست is a nominal sentence: این (this) + بزرگ (great) + آرزوست (آرزو + است) = 'this is a great wish.'
segments.s658-g220: بر here is used in the sense 'according to' / 'befitting' (بر اندازه = 'in proportion to'). اندازه = 'measure/degree'. مرد آزاده‌خوست is an adjectival phrase 'a man of آزاده‌خو (free/noble disposition)'; آزاده‌خوست functions as a characterizing adjective ('magnanimous/freeborn'). The whole phrase qualifies the preceding 'wish' ('befitting a man of noble character').
segments.s658-g221: ولیکن = 'but/yet.' به رنج تو = 'on account of your suffering' (به as a preposition marking cause or relation, رنج + تو 'your suffering'). اندر = 'in/within' and خورست is an archaic/passive-like form meaning 'is eaten/consumed' (خورَدن) — together meaning the thing (the speech/wish) is damaged or consumed by your grief. Word order places the cause before the verb for emphasis.
segments.s658-g222: سخن is the subject 'the speech/words.' گرچه is concessive 'although.' از introduces a comparative relation 'than/from' and پایگه = 'base/foundation.' برترست is the predicate 'is superior' (برتر + است). The clause reads 'although the speech is superior to its base/foundation.'
segments.s658-g223: به بوزرجمهر آن زمان شاه گفت is classical inversion: literal order 'to Bozorgmehr, at that time, the king said' = شاه به بوزرجمهر در آن زمان گفت. به marks the indirect object/recipient (to Bozorgmehr); آن زمان = 'at that time'; گفت is the verb 'said.'
segments.s658-g224: که introduces reported speech ('that'). این آرزو را: این آرزو is the definite object 'this wish' and را is the definite/object marker. نشاید is an impersonal/modal verb 'it is not fitting/should not' and نهفت (from نهفتن) is the infinitive/past-stem meaning 'to hide' or 'be hidden.' Together نشاید نهفت = 'it must not be hidden' / 'ought not to be concealed.'
segments.s658-g33: بدو گفت شاه is a typical classical Persian word order: indirect object (بدو, ‘to him’) + past verb گفت (‘said’) + subject شاه (‘the king’) — literally “to him said the king.” این نشاید بدن is an impersonal negative judgment: این ‘this’ + نشاید (‘it is not proper/should not’) + بدن, here an archaic infinitive sense meaning ‘to do’ (i.e. “this should not be done”). The clause as a whole is the king’s admonition and links to the following exception introduced by مگر.
segments.s658-g34: مگر introduces the exception ‘unless/except.’ آزمون را is the definite object (‘the trial/test’) with the object marker را. بباید شدن is a modal construction: بباید (must/ought) + شدن (infinitive/passive auxiliary) gives a sense of necessity in the passive — “must be done / must be carried out.” Together: ‘unless the trial must be carried out.’
segments.s658-g35: ببر is an imperative ‘take!’ (the verb precedes the object in classical commands). نامه من is the direct object ‘my letter.’ بر رای هند literally ‘on/according to the counsel of India’ — بر+رای expresses ‘to/for the opinion/counsel of India’ (i.e. deliver the letter to those who decide in India).
segments.s658-g36: نگر is an imperative ‘look!’ or ‘see!’ that calls the addressee’s attention. تا که باشد introduces a purposive/subjunctive clause (‘so that there may be’), with باشد as the subjunctive ‘may be.’ بت‌آرای هند is an adjectival/epithetic phrase: بت‌آرای ‘idol‑adorned’ describing هند (‘India’/‘the Indians’), i.e. ‘so that India may be idol‑adorned’ (here meaning to observe whether they are idol‑worshippers).
segments.s658-g37: بدین کار is a prepositional phrase ‘for this work/task’ (بدین = به + این, ‘with/to this’). با خویشتن indicates accompaniment ‘with yourself’ (با + emphatic reflexive خویشتن). یارخواه here functions as a directive to obtain allies/companions (literally ‘friend‑seeker’), i.e. ‘seek companions’ for the task.
segments.s658-g38: همه یاری is ‘all help/every possible aid’ (همه + یاری). از بخت expresses the source ‘from fortune/fate.’ بیدار خواه is a two‑word command: بیدار ‘awake/alert’ + خواه (imperative of خواستن) used here as ‘seek/ask’ or hortative; the sense is roughly ‘solicit alert support from fortune’ or more smoothly, ‘call on every aid and be alert.’
segments.s658-g39: اگر introduces a conditional ‘if.’ نوشگفتی is an adjective meaning ‘wondrous/delightful’ modifying an implied ‘saying/occurrence.’ شود is the present/subjunctive/potential ‘should happen’ or ‘comes to pass.’ در جهان simply places the event ‘in the world.’ Together: ‘If a wondrous thing should happen in the world…’
segments.s658-g40: که introduces the content clause ‘that…’ این گفته ‘this statement/said thing’ is the subject; رمزی describes it as ‘secret/symbolic.’ بود is the past copula ‘was.’ درنهان (classical form در نهان) means ‘in private / in secrecy’ — the clause says that the foregoing remark was of a secret or hidden kind.
segments.s658-g257: جهاندار is the addressee/subject 'world-ruler' (a vocative/nominal subject). تا جاودان is an adverbial duration phrase ('until/ever, eternal') modifying the optative that follows. زنده‌باد is an optative/exclamatory verb phrase ('May he live!') — a fixed poetic formula; word order places the duration phrase before the optative.
segments.s658-g258: زمان و زمین are the grammatical subject(s) 'time and earth'. پیش او is a prepositional phrase 'in the presence of / before him' placed after the subject for emphasis. بنده باد is a predicative noun plus the optative particle باد ('may they be servants'); the optative follows the predicate as a wish.
segments.s658-g259: از اندیشه is a prepositional phrase 'from/out of thought' that gives the cause. دل را uses the object marker را to mark 'the heart' as the affected object. مدار here functions as a prohibitive/imperative ('do not make / do not let'), an archaic/poetic verbal form; ایچ تنگ is a negative adverb + adjective ('not at all narrow'), with ایچ intensifying the negation and تنگ describing the state of the heart.
segments.s658-g260: که is the subordinating conjunction introducing the reason/explanation ('for/that'). دوری تو از روزگار groups together 'your separation from fate/time' (دوری = separation/distance; تو is the possessor/subject). درنگ is a noun meaning 'delay/tarrying' (here: lasting a long time); the clause states that this separation will not be of long duration, explaining why the heart should not be narrowed.
segments.s658-g261: گهی ... گهی is a correlative adverbial pair meaning 'sometimes ... sometimes' expressing alternation. بر + noun (فراز / نشیب) is a prepositional construction 'on the summit / on the descent' (i.e. 'on high / in low places'); the two halves are parallel and coordinate.
segments.s658-g262: Again a pair with گهی ... گهی 'sometimes ... sometimes'. با + noun ('with') indicates accompaniment or circumstance: با مراد 'with fulfilment/desire' and با نهیب 'with rebuke/shock'; the construction lists alternating conditions of fortune.
segments.s658-g263: ازین دو (contraction of از این دو) means 'of these two'. یکی نیز here means 'not even one' (نیز used emphatically with the negative that follows). جاوید نیست is the predicate 'is not eternal' (جاوید = eternal, نیست = does not exist/be everlasting), so the whole segment states that neither of the two states endures.
segments.s658-g264: به بودن is a circumstantial gerundial phrase 'by/with the being' i.e. 'while [you] exist'. تو را with را marks the person affected ('for you' / 'concerning you'). راه امید نیست is an existential negation 'there is no way/path of hope' (نیست negates existence). Together: as long as you are, there remains no road of hope.
segments.s658-g1: نگه کن is an imperative 'behold/look' (نگه = 'gaze', کن = 'do!' imperative). The conjunction/particle که introduces the embedded clause. The phrase شادان برزین places the epithet شادان ('joyful') before the proper name برزین (poetic ordering), and the clause ends with the past verb گفت ('said'), with چه as the interrogative 'what' inside the reported-question structure ('see what Barzin said').
segments.s658-g2: بدانگه is an adverb meaning 'at that moment/thereupon'. The particle که again introduces a subordinate/result clause. بگشاد here is an archaic/poetic past form (be- + گشاد) meaning 'he opened' or 'revealed'. راز is the direct object 'secret', and از نهفت is a prepositional phrase 'out of concealment' (از + نهفت 'hidden') describing how the secret was disclosed.
segments.s658-g3: بدرگه is a locative/prepositional compound meaning 'at the court' or 'in the presence of the court'. شهنشاه is the title 'king of kings' (emperor). نوشین‌روان is an epithet 'sweet‑souled' placed after the title in apposition; Persian poetic style often strings title + epithet together as a single nominal identification.
segments.s658-g4: که introduces a relative clause 'whose'. نامش is 'his name' (نام + enclitic -ش 'his'). بماناد is an optative/jussive poetic form meaning 'may remain' (a wish or benediction). تا is temporal 'until', and جاودان is 'eternal' — together 'may his name remain until eternity' (i.e. forever).
segments.s658-g5: ز here is the poetic preposition 'from'. هر دانشی = 'every kind/branch of knowledge' (هر + دانشی). موبدی is 'a mobed' (Zoroastrian priest) and خواستی is a past tense verb 'he desired/asked for' (past 3rd‑person in context); the phrase uses a fronted prepositional phrase (ز هر دانشی) for emphasis — a common poetic inversion.
segments.s658-g6: که introduces the purpose/result clause: 'so that / that'. درگه is 'the court' (object/place). بدیشان is the dative/benefactive 'for them' (ب + ایشان). بیاراستی is a poetic/old form of آراستن (to arrange/adorn/set up) used here as a past or purposive form 'he furnished/established for them' — together, 'that he might set up a court for them.'
segments.s658-g7: This segment begins a coordinated list of person‑types. پزشک is 'physician', سخنگوی is 'speaker' or 'spokesman' (an epithet that may modify or stand parallel to پزشک), و is the coordinating 'and', and کنداوران (plural) are 'investigators' or 'excavators' — here a list of skilled roles summoned to the court. The nouns are presented in a poetic catalogue, so modifiers may appear after or alongside the nouns.
segments.s658-g8: بزرگان is 'the great ones' or 'nobles'. وکارآزموده is a compound token meaning 'and experienced' / 'well‑tried' (here modifying the following noun), and سران is 'leaders' or 'chiefs'. Together they form the second half of the catalogue: 'the great and the experienced leaders.'
segments.s658-g161: هر آن در که is a temporal concessive clause meaning “whenever/at the moment that.” در here is part of the classical formula هر آن در که (literary word order) followed by a subordinate verb. از + نامه marks the source/content read (“from the letter”), and برخواندی is the poetic past/imperfect of خواندن with the prefix بر- meaning “to read aloud/recite”; together the clause = “whenever (he) read from the letter.”
segments.s658-g162: همه روز is an adverbial phrase “all day/throughout the day.” بر دل is a prepositional phrase meaning “upon the heart” (showing the emotional effect). همی‑راندی is the habitual/imperfect form with the older particle همی‑ plus the past-ending ‑دی, giving a repeated or continuous past meaning “would drive/press” or “kept driving/pressing.”
segments.s658-g163: ز نامه فزون places the source first (from the letter) and fuzun (فزون) means “more/greater” — here ‘the letter produced something greater’ or ‘gave him still more.’ ز آنک here introduces a causal/explanatory clause (“for/since”), بودیش is the classical past form (بود + ‑یش enclitic) meaning “it was” / “it belonged,” and یاد is the noun “memory/remembrance” — together: “for its remembrance was his.”
segments.s658-g164: ز برخواندی نیز is continuative: ز here = “from/because of,” برخواندی repeats the verbal idea “from the (act of) reading aloud,” and نیز = “also/too.” تا بامداد is a temporal phrase “until dawn/morning.” Together: “and from the readings as well, (it stayed) until morning.”
segments.s658-g165: همی‌بود is the continuous/past habitual copular verb (همی‑ + بود) — “he was / continued to be.” شادان followed by دل (شادان دل) is an adjectival phrase 'joyful of heart'; و تن درست joins a parallel description 'and of a sound/proper body' (درست here = ‘sound/proper’). The order is adjective + noun, a common poetic pattern.
segments.s658-g166: بدانش is a compact classical connective meaning “because of that / on that account” (بدان + enclitic ش). همی is the habitual/emphatic particle, جان روشن is a noun phrase 'bright/illuminated soul' (جان = soul/life, روشن = bright/enlightened), and بشست is the simple past of شستن 'he washed' — idiomatically here 'he would cleanse/enlighten his soul.'
segments.s658-g167: چو = 'when' introduces a temporal clause. زو is the contracted classical form of ز او 'from him.' نامه رفتی uses رفتن in passive/impersonal sense 'the letter went/was sent' and به شاه جهان is the dative/goal 'to the king of the world' (شاه جهان = lofty epithet for the king). Word order reflects classical poetic inversion.
segments.s658-g168: دری here names the language/dialect (Dari/Persian) and begins the object phrase 'a Dari version of Kalila.' از کلیله is 'of Kalila,' نبشتی is the past of نوشتن 'he wrote/inscribed,' and نهان placed at the end functions adverbially 'secretly/hiddenly.' The verb-final ordering and the adverb at the end are typical of the poetic register.
segments.s658-g249: گزارنده را پیش بنشاندند is a past narrative clause in which گزارنده (the messenger) is marked by the object marker را and is placed before the verb for emphasis. پیش here means 'in front of' / 'in the presence of', and بنشاندند is 3rd-person plural past ('they seated/placed'). The implied subject 'they' carries over to the following verb.
segments.s658-g250: همه نامه‌بر رودکی خواندند: خواندند is also 3rd-person plural past ('they read/recited') and the subject is the same 'they'. همه here means 'all' or 'everyone' and scopes over what follows. نامه‌بر is the noun 'letter‑carrier / envoy' (used here as the thing bearing the letters), رودکی names the source (Rudaki) and is the object of خواندند (they read Rudaki's letters); classical Persian can omit a linking particle (را) in such constructions.
segments.s658-g251: بپیوست here functions like 'was attached / was enclosed' (a past or perfect sense used for an appended/attached item). گویا is parenthetical 'it seems / apparently' and modifies the whole clause. پراگنده is an adjective 'scattered/widespread' describing what was attached; the final را marks that phrase as definite/emphatic.
segments.s658-g252: بسفت اینچنین در آگنده را is a compact clause: بسفت is a verb 'they fastened / made firm,' this time active; اینچنین 'thus / in this way' adverbially modifies the action. در means 'in/inside' and آگنده is an adjective 'filled / full' describing the container; را again marks the definite/emphatic object. Word order puts manner (اینچنین) before the locative phrase.
segments.s658-g253: بدان کو سخن راند آرایشست is a classical relative clause. بدان کو = 'to him who / to that one who' (بدان is a compound prepositional/relativizing element + کو). سخن راند is a literary idiom meaning 'uttered words / spoke' (راند here conveys causing/putting forth speech), and آرایشست is a nominal predicate 'it is an ornament' (آرایش = ornament; -ست = 'is').
segments.s658-g254: چو ابله بود جای بخشایشست: چو here = 'when'; ابله = 'a fool'. بود is the past/be auxiliary 'is/was'. جای = 'place/room' and بخشایشست = 'is (for) forgiveness/pardon' (بخشایش = forgiveness; -ست = 'is'). The clause says that where a fool is, there is room for pardon — i.e., foolishness admits pardon.
segments.s658-g255: حدیث پراگنده بپراگند is an imperative/jussive-like phrase: حدیث پراگنده 'the dispersed/widespread tale' is the theme, and بپراگند is a verbal form meaning 'scatter!' or 'be scattered.' In classical diction such a verb form can function as a command or exhortation applied to the tale.
segments.s658-g256: چو پیوسته شد جان و مغز آگند: چو = 'when'; پیوسته = 'joined/connected' (here describing the state of soul and mind); شد = 'became' (past of شدن); جان و مغز = 'life/soul and brain/mind'; آگند = 'filled/replete' (predicate adjective). The clause reads 'when soul and brain became joined, (they) became filled.'
segments.s658-g97: This is an introductory clause: چنين گفت is a formulaic past tense 'thus said'. زان پس = 'after that/thereafter' (زان = 'from that'; پس = 'after/then'). بران is a poetic contraction meaning 'to them' or 'to those' (literally 'بر آن(ان)') and بخردان is a vocative/plural address 'O wise ones' (بخرد = 'wise'). The whole segment sets up direct speech addressed to the wise.
segments.s658-g98: که‌ای begins a following clause describing or addressing the addressees (here functioning like 'who are' or a vocative 'O ...'). کاردیده is a compound adjective 'کاره‌دیده' = 'experienced, battle/feeling‑tested'. ستوده is 'praised' or 'lauded'. ردان here is an epithet in apposition; its exact nuance is irregular in the provided senses, so it functions as another descriptive adjective attached to the vocative phrase.
segments.s658-g99: که introduces a relative/subordinate clause 'that/whom'. دانید is present indicative (2nd plural) 'you know' or 'you consider'. داناتر is the comparative 'more learned/wise'. از خویشتن ('than yourselves') uses از as the comparative marker and خویشتن as the emphatic reflexive 'your own selves'.
segments.s658-g100: کجا 'where' asks for the place/state in which the following predicate holds. سرفرازد is a stative verb 'is exalted/honored' (3rd person). بدین is the preposition ب + demonstrative دین = 'in/at this' (poetic contraction), and انجمن is 'assembly, council'. The phrase asks 'where he is distinguished in this assembly.'
segments.s658-g101: به پاسخ شدند is an idiomatic verbal phrase 'gave an answer / answered' (به + noun پاسخ + شدند 'became' → 'became to an answer' = 'answered'). انجمن همسخن is the subject 'the assembly (whose members are) conversant/speakers' (انجمن = assembly; همسخن = interlocutors/conversants). Word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect.
segments.s658-g102: که introduces the content of their answer ('that'). داننده is 'the knower/expert' (subject of the reported clause). پیرست here reflects the older compound punctuation of پیر است = 'is old' (a copular phrase: 'is an old man' / 'is aged'); ایدر = ایدر/ایدر 'here'; کهن = 'old, ancient' used as an attributive reinforcing 'old'.
segments.s658-g103: به سال و خرد is an ablative/instrumental phrase 'in age and in wisdom' (به + noun). او is the 3rd‑person pronoun 'he'. ز ما is the comparative 'than us' (ز = than/from, ما = we/us). مهترست is a predicate meaning 'is greater/older/superior' (archaic/polite comparative with the copula -ست).
segments.s658-g104: به دانش 'in knowledge' sets the domain. ز هر مهتری 'than every elder/chief' uses ز as the comparative marker and هر + مهتری to mean 'any/each elder'. بهترست is the predicate 'he is better/superior' (3rd‑person present), so the clause states he surpasses all elders in learning.
segments.s658-g145: دژم گشت is a two-word predicate: دژم (an adjective, 'sullen') + گشت (past of گشتن, 'became/turned'), so together 'became sullen'. زان is a contracted/prepositional use 'از + آن' meaning 'from that' or 'because of that', and آرزو ('desire/longing') is the cause. جان شاه literally 'the king's soul/life' (جان modified by شاه), i.e. 'the king's spirit'. Word order follows Persian Modifier→Head for 'جان شاه' and Cause phrase follows the predicate.
segments.s658-g146: بپیچید is here a past/perfective 3rd-person action 'he turned/withdrew' (the b- prefix appears in classical/poetic forms but functions as the simple past here). برخویشتن is a compound adverbial 'بر + خویشتن' meaning 'to/against himself' — i.e. 'into himself' or 'inwardly'. چندگاه is an adverb 'for a while'. The verb governs the following adverbial phrase.
segments.s658-g147: به + proper name (برزوی) marks the indirect object 'to Barzū' ('he said to Barzū'). گفت is the past verb 'said' (the provided lemma senses list does not include the verbal sense, so a direct sense index is not available). این کس 'this person/man' is the topic of the reported clause; از ما نجست is negated past: نجست = ن + جست(ن) → 'did not seek' or 'did not come to (ask of) us', where از ما indicates target 'us'.
segments.s658-g148: نه اکنون نه ... is a correlative negative 'neither ... nor ...'. اکنون = 'now'. از روزگار نخست = 'from the first days / former times' (از = 'from', روزگار = 'time/age', نخست used adverbially = 'originally/at first'). Together they qualify the negation in the previous clause.
segments.s658-g149: ولیکن is a discourse particle 'but/yet'. جهاندار is a title 'sovereign' (noun) and نوشین‌روان is an adjectival epithet 'sweet‑souled / pleasant‑minded' modifying جهاندار; the epithet follows the title as is common in poetic Persian.
segments.s658-g150: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. تن here = 'body' (i.e. the physical person), and بخواهد is the 3rd-person present/subjunctive of خواستن 'he wants/desires' (used in the protasis of the conditional). ز ما = 'from us' (a source/recipient), یا = 'or', روان = 'soul/spirit'. The clause sets a conditional offering/request (the sentence continues after this line).
segments.s658-g151: نداریم is the negative present 'we do not have' (ن- negative + داریم). ازو 'of/from him' indicates possession/source. باز چیزی 'anything left/available' — here باز functions as 'available/left' (poetic use) rather than 'again'. که هست 'that exists/which there is' links to the following conditional pair. Overall: 'we have nothing left of him that exists...'.
segments.s658-g152: A paired conditional repetition: اگر سرفرازست اگر زیردست = 'whether he is sarfraz (exalted/triumphant) or zirdast (low/subordinate)'. سرفرازست is a stative predicate 'is proud/triumphant'; زیردست is an adjective/noun 'subordinate, lowly' or 'one under authority'. The repetition with اگر gives the sense 'in any case, regardless of his rank.'
segments.s658-g25: چو here is the temporal conjunction 'when' placed at the start of the clause. بر + مرده is the prepositional phrase 'upon the dead/corpse' (بر = on/upon, مرده = corpse). بپراگند is a verbal form meaning 'scatters / is scattered' (literary present/optative form), and بی‌گمان is an adverb 'without doubt' that modifies the whole clause. Word order is typical classical Persian: temporal conjunction → prepositional phrase → verb → adverbial qualifier.
segments.s658-g26: سخنگوی is the predicative noun 'speaker/spokesman' (the role that will be assumed). گردد is the 3rd person singular literary verb 'becomes' (گرد + ات = گردد; here simple present/subjunctive used narratively). هم functions as 'even/also' emphasizing the unexpectedness, and اندر زمان is an adverbial phrase 'in (its) time / in the age' (اندر = in, زمان = time) modifying the verbal event.
segments.s658-g27: کنون من puts the temporal adverb 'now' before the pronoun 'I' (topic/fronting). بدستوری is a compound prepositional/nominal phrase 'by the order/command of' (بدست = by the hand/under, -وری from دستور = order), and شهریار is the genitive/agent 'the king' specifying whose command it is. The whole phrase functions as a circumstantial agent for the verb in the next segment.
segments.s658-g28: بپیمایم is the 1st person singular literary present/subjunctive of پیمودن 'to traverse/undertake' (بـ- prefix + present stem), here 'I will/shall undertake'. این راه is the direct object 'this road/path' (demonstrative + noun), and دشوار خوار are two postpositive adjectives 'difficult' and 'despised/lowly' modifying راه. Adjectives follow the noun in Persian literary word order.
segments.s658-g29: بسی here is an intensifier 'much/abundant' modifying دانشی 'knowledge' (note the ezāfe-like apposition in classical style where the modifier follows). رهنمای attaches to دانشی as a qualifier meaning 'guiding' or 'advisory' (a compound sense 'guiding knowledge'). آورم is the 1st person present/subjunctive of آوردن 'I bring / I will bring', stating the speaker's intention.
segments.s658-g30: مگر here introduces an exception/conditional 'unless' (linking to the previous clause). کین شگفتی is the noun phrase 'this astonishing kīn' where کین primarily means 'vengeance/hatred' and شگفتی 'astonishment/wonder' qualifies it; together they mean 'this astonishing vengeance'. بجای آورم literally 'I bring in its place' (بجای = instead/in place of; آورم = I bring), so the clause means 'unless I bring this marvel/vengeance instead.'
segments.s658-g31: تن is the noun 'body' (subject). مرده‌گر here is a compound noun and functions as an epithet modifying تن (literary usages vary between 'undertaker/gravedigger' or 'that which is dead‑making'); together the phrase reads 'the (dead-)body'. زنده گردد is the 3rd person singular literary verb phrase 'become alive' (زنده = alive, گردد = becomes). رواست is a fused predicate meaning 'it is fitting/permissible' (روا + است compressed to رواست), so the clause states a judgment about the event.
segments.s658-g32: که begins an explanatory/subordinate clause 'that/for' giving the reason. نوشین‌روان is the subject phrase 'the sweet‑souled one' (an epithet), بر جهان is the prepositional phrase 'over the world' (بر = over, جهان = world), and پادشاست is a literary fused verb/noun 'is king / is ruler' (پادشاه + است contracted), stating the sovereign status that justifies the preceding judgment.
segments.s658-g225: نویسنده is the subject 'the scribe/author' (here a copyist). از کلک governs the material/source ('from the reed/kolak'); چون introduces a temporal/comparative clause 'when/as'. خامه is the result noun 'a quill/pen', and کرد is the simple past 'made/did' — together: 'the scribe, when he turned the reed into a quill (made a pen)'.
segments.s658-g226: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of'; برزوی is the proper name Barzū in genitive. یک is the numeral 'one/a single'. در سر is a locative phrase 'in/at the head/top (of)'; نامه is 'letter/document'; کرد is the past verb 'made/wrote' — overall: 'he made/wrote one (entry/heading) of Barzū at the head of the letter.'
segments.s658-g227: نبشت is the archaic past form 'wrote/inscribed' (classical for نوشت). او is the third‑person pronoun 'he'. بران is a prepositional adverb 'on/upon that'; نامه is 'letter/document'; خسروی is an adjective 'royal/kingly' — together: 'he wrote/inscribed on that royal letter.'
segments.s658-g228: نبود is the past negative/existential 'there was not/was not'. آن زمان is the temporal phrase 'that time/then'. خط is 'script/handwriting'. جز is the exception marker 'except'. پهلوی names the Pahlavi script/language — so: 'at that time there was no script except Pahlavi.'
segments.s658-g229: همی‌بود is an imperfective/stative past 'continued to be / remained'. با ارج is an instrumental/qualifying phrase 'with honor / in esteem'. در گنج شاه is the locative 'in the king's treasure/treasury' — yielding 'he remained honored in the king’s treasury.'
segments.s658-g230: بدو is a dative 'to him'. ناسزا is 'insult/abuse' (here 'an insulting act/word/look'). کس in this idiom means 'no one' (negative indefinite). نکردی is the past negative 'did not do' (classical form used for 'no one did'). نگاه is 'a look / glance' and functions as the object: 'no one cast an insulting glance at him.'
segments.s658-g231: چنین 'thus/so' sets the manner. تا is the temporal 'until'. بتازی derives from the verb 'to raid/attack' and here functions as a time/event ('until the attack/assault' or 'until the Tazi raid'). سخن 'speech/words' (here general discourse/utterance) and راندند 'they drove/uttered/put forth' — together: 'they kept speaking/uttering words until the attack.'
segments.s658-g232: ورا is the dative 'to him'. پهلوانی is the noun 'heroism / hero (i.e., champion)' used as a predicate/title. همی‌خواندند is a habitual/past plural 'they called/sang (of him)' — together: 'they called him a champion / sang of his heroism.'
segments.s658-g201: چرا رفتی is a direct question: چرا 'why' + رفتی past 2nd singular 'you went/left'. The vocative ای رنج‌دیده follows the verb and addresses the addressee ('O one who has suffered'). ز گنج is a prepositional phrase using the archaic/poetic preposition ز (= از) meaning 'from/away from the treasure'.
segments.s658-g202: This clause asks whether treasure is appropriate for a certain person: کسی را سزد is an impersonal construction 'it befits someone' (سزد = 'it befits/ought'). گنج is the thing in question. کو here is a classical/poetic relative/connector (not the locative 'where'); دید is past 'has seen' and رنج is 'suffering/hardship' — together 'who has seen hardship'.
segments.s658-g203: چنین 'thus' modifies the verb آورد (past 3rd sing.), giving 'thus answered'. پاسـخ آورد is a fixed phrase 'gave an answer'; برزو is the subject (the speaker). بشاه is a vocative 'O king!' attached to the clause—classical word order places the vocative after the clause.
segments.s658-g204: که introduces reported speech ('that' / 'that is,'); ای is the vocative particle 'O' addressing the king; تاج تو is 'your crown' with the possessive pronoun تو following the noun. برتر از is the comparative 'higher/superior to' and چرخ ماه is the object of comparison (classical poetic wording: 'the wheel/firmament of the moon'). Note the copula 'is' is omitted (typical in Persian).
segments.s658-g205: هرآنکس که introduces a free relative 'whoever/whosoever'. The pronoun او is a classical repetition of the third person referring back to that antecedent (emphatic or resumptive). پوشش شاه is 'the king's covering/robe' (پوشش = garment/protection) and یافت is past 3rd singular 'obtained/received'.
segments.s658-g206: به بخت و به تخت = 'to/into fortune and to/onto the throne' (repetition of به for both objects). مهی is an adjectival modifier 'moon‑like, beautiful' qualifying تخت. راه یافت is a verbal phrase 'found a way' or idiomatically 'gained access/attained' (یافت as past 3rd sing.).
segments.s658-g207: دگر signals contrast ('but/other'), and آنک is a relative 'who/that' introducing the clause. با جامه شهریار is a prepositional phrase 'with the garment of the sovereign' — i.e., 'wearing the king's robe' (جامه = garment, شهریار = king/sovereign). Classical Persian often puts possessor after the noun.
segments.s658-g208: ببیند here is a present (3rd person) form 'he sees' (classical narrative uses present forms like this). مرا (م + را) is the object 'me' (م as clitic + را object marker), and because Persian often omits a verb like 'calls/considers', the noun phrase مرد ناسازگار functions as the characterization: 'he will see/regard me as an ill‑suited/discordant man.' The copular idea is implied.
segments.s658-g81: This is a prepositional/type phrase built with the poetic preposition ز (here = 'from/of') introducing the noun phrase هرگونه دارو ('every/any kind of remedy'). The repeated ز before خشک and تر is a poetic emphatic pattern meaning 'of the dry and of the moist' (i.e. 'dry and moist remedies'). Adjectives follow in the order خشک و تر.
segments.s658-g82: همی is an archaic/poetic iterative adverb (here marking continuous or habitual action: 'were scattering' / 'kept scattering'). پراگند functions as the verbal/adjectival notion 'scattered', and the sequence بر مرده بر uses the preposition بر ('upon') poetically repeated to show direction/target: 'upon the dead'. Word order (adverb + predicate + prepositional phrase) is typical of epic Persian.
segments.s658-g83: یکی here functions idiomatically with the negative verb نگشت to mean 'not a single' (i.e. 'no one'). نگشت is the past of گشت in the sense 'became' (here negated: 'did not become'), and از گیا marks the instrument/source 'by/from the herb'. The clause literally reads 'not one dead became alive from the herb.'
segments.s658-g84: همانا که is an emphatic introducer ('verily/for indeed, that…'). سست آمد is a predicative construction where آمد ('came') takes سست ('weak/ineffective') as a complement — an idiom meaning 'proved ineffective'. آن کیمیا is the subject 'that kimiya' (kimiya here = the miraculous elixir/secret remedy).
segments.s658-g85: همه کوه is the object 'all the mountains' (i.e. 'every mountain'). بسپرد is the past verb 'he entrusted/assigned', and یک‌یک modifies the following verbal notion بپای ('to stand/watch one by one') — a compact phrase meaning 'he set them, one by one, to keep watch.' The form بپای is a poetic/preverbal present-stem used as an infinitival/resultative complement.
segments.s658-g86: ابر رنج او is a genitive phrase 'a cloud (i.e. mass) of his suffering/toil.' بر نیامد بجای combines the negated past نیامد ('did not come') with بجای ('in place of' / 'instead'), so the clause means 'a cloud of his toil did not come in its stead' (i.e. it did not substitute or appear in place of what was needed).
segments.s658-g87: بدانست is simple past 'he knew' or 'he realized.' کان is an archaic/poetic particle here functioning like a demonstrative/complementizer 'that' introducing the content of his knowledge. کار آن پادشاست literally 'the work/act was the king's' — پادشاست here indicates 'is of the king' / 'belongs to the king' in the sense of authorship or responsibility.
segments.s658-g88: که introduces an explanatory/relative clause describing the king: زنده است is present 'is alive,' followed by predicates جاوید ('eternal') and فرمانرواست ('is sovereign / rules'). The present-tense predicates state enduring qualities of the king; the coordination is paratactic (adjectives stacked with و).
segments.s658-g169: بدین چاره is an adverbial phrase: بدین (به+این, here written poetically) = 'by this' or 'with this', and چاره = 'means/expedient'. Together they form 'by this means'. Note the classical compressed spelling; the prepositional prefix ب- is attached to این.
segments.s658-g170: فرستاد is the simple past verb 'he sent'. نزدیک here functions as a spatial/adverbial phrase 'near/to (the court of)' and نوشین‌روان is a descriptive name 'sweet‑souled' — together نزدیک نوشین‌روان indicates the destination/recipient (toward Nushin‑ravan). The verb precedes this adverbial phrase in the line for metrical order but is the main action of the clause.
segments.s658-g171: بدین گونه = 'in this manner/way' (بدین = 'by/with this', گونه = 'way, manner'). تا here introduces the temporal span ('until' / 'when' in older usage) linking to دید 'he saw' — پاسخ‌نامه is the direct object 'the reply‑letter' and دید is the past verb 'he saw/received'. The order places the adverbial phrase up front for emphasis.
segments.s658-g172: که introduces a reported/content clause 'that ...'. دریای دانش is a genitival phrase 'the sea (ocean) of knowledge' (دریای = دریای + ـِ (construct), دانش = 'knowledge'). بر ما رسید uses بر + pronoun ما + past رسید to mean 'reached us / came upon us' — بر here indicates direction/impact (came upon).
segments.s658-g173: ز ایوان بیامد = 'he came from the iwan/portico' (ز = from, ایوان = iwan, بیامد = past 'came'). به نزدیک رای = 'to near the council/assembly' — به + نزدیک = direction toward proximity, رای = 'counsel/assembly/plan' here 'council'. The phrase indicates movement from the porch toward the council.
segments.s658-g174: بدستوری is an adverbial/ablative phrase 'by (the) command' (بدستور + ی suffixed for adverbial sense). بازگشتن is the infinitive 'to return', here used in narrative to report the action 'returned' (classical Persian often uses the infinitive or masdar form to narrate a past action). به جای = 'to (his) place'. The whole cluster means 'by command, (he) returned to his place.'
segments.s658-g175: چو = 'when'. بگشاد is a past/adjectival form 'opened' (دل بگشاد = 'his heart opened' = he was moved or became willing). رای is the object 'counsel/plan', and بنواختش is a past verb with the enclitic -ش (object 'it/him'): بنواخت = 'struck/played/sounded' used figuratively = 'he voiced/struck forth the counsel'. The enclitic refers back to رای (the thing expressed).
segments.s658-g176: یکی = 'a certain' / 'one' (here introducing a gift). خلعت هندویی = 'an Indian (هندویی) robe of honour' (خلعت = robe of honour, هندویی = Indian‑style). ساختش is the past verb ساخت + enclitic -ش 'made/gave it to him' — in courtly Persian ساختن commonly means 'to bestow/give' in the context of investiture. The enclitic points to the robe as the object.
segments.s658-g57: نباشد is a negative subjunctive/impersonal form used here idiomatically as “it is not (a) wonder.” شگفت is the predicate noun “a wonder/marvel.” از + جهاندار پاک links the source/agent or cause: “of/from the pure world‑ruler” (literally “no wonder from the pure sovereign”).
segments.s658-g58: که‌گر is an archaic clausal conjunction (که گر = “that if/that when” or “that he who”). مردگان را uses را as the definite/direct‑object marker. برآرد is a present/subjunctive third‑person verb meaning “bring forth/raise up.” ز خاک is a poetic order for “from the earth/dust.” The relative clause modifies the preceding phrase (i.e. it’s not surprising of the ruler that he would raise the dead).
segments.s658-g59: برهمن (Brahmin) is fronted as the topical noun. بکوه اندرون is a locative phrase ‘in/within the mountain’ (بـ + کوه + اندرون). هرک هست is an older fused form of 'هر که هست' = ‘whoever he is’; هست is the copula/existential verb “is/exists.” Together: ‘a Brahmin is within the mountain, whoever that may be.’
segments.s658-g60: یکی is an indefinite subject ‘someone.’ دارد is the present-tense verb ‘has/holds.’ این رای را is the object with را marking definiteness (‘this opinion/plan’). با تو دست is an idiomatic prepositional phrase: با = ‘with,’ تو = ‘you,’ and دست used figuratively as ‘hand/help/support,’ so با تو دست = ‘is on your side / lends you support.’
segments.s658-g61: بت‌آرای is a compound adjective ‘idol‑adorning/idol‑decorated’ placed before the noun. و فرخنده links a second adjective ‘and auspicious,’ both modifying دستور (order/command). من is the possessor ‘my’ placed after the noun in Persian order: ‘my command.’
segments.s658-g62: هم = ‘also/likewise’ introduces a parallel item. آن گنج = ‘that treasure.’ و links the compound modifier پرمایه گنجور (پرمایه = abundant/substantial; گنجور = treasure‑laden/treasury), and من again marks possession ‘my.’ The whole phrase lists another possession: ‘that treasure, my abundant treasury.’
segments.s658-g63: بد و نیک are coordinated opposites ‘bad and good’ that modify هندوستان (Hindustan/India). پیش here means ‘before/in front of (you)’ and تست is used in the sense of trial/examination; the phrase says that India’s good and bad stand before you as a test/trial.
segments.s658-g64: بزرگی مرا = ‘my greatness’ with مرا the accusative/possessive object; در کم و بیش = ‘in small and great (matters)’ (در = ‘in,’ کم = ‘small/few,’ بیش = ‘greater/more’). تست here is the verb ‘you test/examine’ (addressing the addressee); literally: ‘you test my greatness in lesser and greater matters.’
segments.s658-g89: دلش (del‑esh) is the possessed noun 'his heart' (دل + enclitic ش). گشت (gašt) is the past of گشتن used as 'became/turned' and introduces the resulting state expressed by the predicate سوزان (an adjective 'burning'). ز here is causal ('because of') and links that state to the following noun phrase تشویر شاه 'the king's تشویر' (a noun phrase where تشویر is the thing causing the heart to burn).
segments.s658-g90: هم ... هم ... is the correlative construction 'both ... and ...' that coordinates two causes. Each از is the preposition introducing the causal source ('because of'/'from'): از نامداران = 'from/because of the renowned (men)' and از رنج راه = 'from/because of the hardship of the road' (رنج + راه is a genitive/attributive sequence 'hardship of the way').
segments.s658-g91: وزان is a fused connective meaning 'and from that' or 'and because of that' tying this clause to what went before. خواسته is a noun 'the desire/wish' (here the thing that was concealed). نیز 'also' reinforces that the action applied as well. کاورده بود is a compound past ('کاورده' = 'covered/veiled' + auxiliary بود) producing a past perfective sense 'had covered/hidden'.
segments.s658-g92: ز is causal 'because of'. گفتار بیهوده is a noun phrase 'vain/pointless speech' (گفتار = speech, بیهوده = worthless/pointless). آزرده بود is the predicate 'was distressed/annoyed' (آزرده = past participial adjective 'distressed', بود = past 'was'). The clause states the emotional effect of such speech.
segments.s658-g93: ز introduces the cause 'because of'. کار نبشته is a noun phrase where نبشته functions adjectivally/nominally with کار ('the written/inscribed deed' or 'that which was written'); ببد is an older/poetic past form of 'became/was' and here conveys 'became/turned', and تنگدل is a compound adjective 'narrow‑hearted / close‑hearted', describing the resulting disposition. Word order is causal phrase → verb → resulting adjective.
segments.s658-g94: که introduces an explanatory clause 'for/since/that' giving the reason: آن مرد 'that man' is the subject, بیدانش is an adjective 'ignorant' (literally 'without knowledge'), و is 'and', and سنگدل is 'hard‑hearted'. The clause explains why the speaker became تنگدل.
segments.s658-g95: چرا is the interrogative 'why'. خیره 'staring/astonished' modifies an implied subject (he) and precedes the locative/goal phrase بر باد 'toward/at the wind' (بر = onto/upon, باد = wind). چیزی نبشت is the verb phrase 'wrote something' (نبشت = wrote). The line is a rhetorical question: why did he, staring at the wind, write anything?
segments.s658-g96: که here gives the reason/explanation 'for/since'. بد بار is a collocation 'bad/ill burden' (بد = bad, بار = burden). آن رنج گفتار زشت is a noun phrase 'that pain/offense of ugly/vile speech' (رنج = harm/offense, گفتار = speech, زشت = vile/ugly). The clause explains why writing (to the wind) was foolish: the speech would impose an evil burden.
segments.s658-g185: بگفت is the past narrative verb 'he said' (verb before the phrase). آنچ از رای دید و شنید is a free relative: آنچ 'that which' + از رای 'of/from (the) counsel' + two past verbs دید و شنید 'saw and heard' listing the things he reported. Word order is classical Persian: verb (بگفت) followed by the content of the speech as a noun phrase.
segments.s658-g186: بجای 'in place of/instead of' introduces a contrastive prepositional phrase (bajāy). گیا is 'vegetation/plant(s)'. آمد پدید is a biclausal verbal sequence meaning 'came forth/appeared' (آمد + پدید together produce the sense 'appeared, came into view'). The whole clause reads 'in place of plants, knowledge appeared.'
segments.s658-g187: بدو گفت is 'he said to him' (بدو = to him; گفت here functions as the reporting verb). شاه‌ای پسندیده مرد is vocative/address: شاه‌ای 'O king' or 'O sovereign' (here vocative particle -ای follows شاه), followed by پسندیده مرد 'esteemed/pleasing man' — an epithet addressing the addressee. Classical word order allows the indirect object marker (بدو) before the reporting verb.
segments.s658-g188: کلیله is the subject/name (here a personal name). روان مرا is a possessed/affected noun phrase: روان 'spirit/soul' with مرا functioning as the clitic pronoun referring to the speaker ('my' / 'me' as object), and را is the classical definite/object marker attached after the pronoun. زنده کرد is a causative/past construction 'made alive / revived' (کرد as 'caused/made' with زنده as predicate). Together: 'Kalīleh revived my spirit' or 'Kalīleh made my soul live.'
segments.s658-g189: تو اکنون gives the addressee and time 'you now'. ز گنجور is an ablative/locative 'from the treasury/treasury-house'. بستان here functions as an imperative 'take!' (classical/poetic imperative form), and کلید is the direct object 'the key'. Word order is typical imperative: addressee + time, prepositional phrase, imperative verb, object.
segments.s658-g190: ز چیزی که ... is a relative clause: ز چیزی 'from something' / 'of the things' with که introducing the relative 'which/that'. باید بباید گزید stacks two modal auxiliaries (باید and بباید) for necessity/intensity followed by گزید 'choose' (past-stem form used here in a modal sense). The clause means 'choose that which ought to be chosen' or 'from those things choose what must be chosen.'
segments.s658-g191: بیامد is the past tense 'he came'. خرد یافته is a nominal/adjectival phrase 'one who has obtained wisdom' (خرد 'wisdom' + یافته 'acquired/found' used postpositively). سوی گنج is the goal phrase 'toward the treasure.' Note classical verb–subject or verb–modifier orders allow the participial phrase after the verb.
segments.s658-g192: به گنجور is a prepositional phrase 'at/to the treasury-house'. بسیار is an adverb 'greatly / much' modifying the verb. ننمود (نمود) is the past 'displayed/shown' and رنج is the direct object 'toil/hardship'. The clause thus says he 'showed much toil at the treasury' (i.e., he undertook/manifested much effort there).
segments.s658-g65: بیاراستندش is a past (3rd-person plural) verb form meaning 'they arrayed/arranged/adorned him'; the clitic -ش marks the 3rd-person singular object 'him'. The following prepositional phrase به نزدیک رای places the action 'near the council/assembly' (به + نزدیک + رای), with نزدیک modifying رای.
segments.s658-g66: یکی نامور is an indefinite nominal phrase: یکی (a certain/someone) + اسمِ وصفی نامور (renowned), i.e. 'a renowned one'. چون here is the conjunction 'as' (compare 'like/as'), linking to ببایست جای, where ببایست (from باید) expresses that something 'was fitting/was necessary' and جای ('place/position') names what is being respected.
segments.s658-g67: خورشگر فرستاد هم خوردنی has the verb فرستاد (past, 3rd‑singular) 'sent'; خورشگر and خوردنی are two objects coordinated by هم ('also'), so the sense is 'he sent a cook and also food'. In Persian the verb follows its objects here (or the utterance omits the overt subject, carried from context).
segments.s658-g68: همان پوشش نغز و گستردنی is a noun phrase: همان (that/the same) + پوشش (cloak/covering) followed by two descriptive adjectives نغز (fine/delightful) and گستردنی (lit. 'spreadable' or 'wide‑spreading'), joined by و. In classical/poetic word order adjectives commonly follow the noun they modify.
segments.s658-g69: برفت آن شب و رای زد با ردان begins with برفت, an archaic/prefixal past meaning 'he went/left' (here treated as an irregular past form). آن شب is a temporal phrase 'that night'. رای زد is a verbal collocation (رای زدن) meaning 'to deliberate / take counsel' (زد is past 3rd singular). با introduces the companions of the deliberation (با + ردان = 'with ...'); word order places the time phrase and the coordinated verb phrase after the verb.
segments.s658-g70: بزرگان قنوج با بخردان is a sequence of two companion phrases. بزرگان قنوج = 'the great ones/chiefs of Kannauj', where قنوج (a place name) is attached to بزرگان by the implied genitive relation. با بخردان = 'with the wise ones' (بخردان is plural, 'the wise'), introduced by the preposition با.
segments.s658-g71: چو is the conjunction 'when'. برزد is an archaic past (3rd‑singular) meaning 'rose/appeared' (poetic). سر از کوه 'head from the mountain' is a poetic locution ('the head/top emerging from the mountain'), where سر (summit/top) + از (from) + کوه (mountain) describes the origin. رخشنده روز is a compound describing 'the radiant day' (رخشنده qualifies روز).
segments.s658-g72: پدید آمد is a light‑verb expression meaning 'appeared' (پدید 'appear' + آمد 'came'). آن is the demonstrative 'that' modifying شمع (candle), and گیتی‌فروز is a compound epithet 'world‑illuminating' that follows the noun in typical poetic style: 'that candle which lights the world'.
segments.s658-g17: چنین گفت is a fixed narrative formula meaning “thus he said,” with گفت (past of گفتن) as the main verb. کای is the archaic vocative “O,” placed before شاه “king”; دانش‌پذیر follows شاه as an epithet (post‑nominal adjective/compound noun) meaning “receptive of knowledge.” Word order (formula + vocative + title + epithet) is typical of epic Persian.
segments.s658-g18: پژوهنده is another epithet—‘inquirer’/‘seeker’—applied to the addressee; و links epithets. یافته is a past participle ‘acquired/obtained’ that here modifies the following noun یادگیر, so the phrase reads roughly ‘(one) who has acquired and is a learner’ or more smoothly ‘an earnest learner of what he has found.’ The sequence (participle + noun) is a poetic way to form a compound description.
segments.s658-g19: من امروز is an emphatic subject + time adverb (“I today” = “today I”). در + دفتر هندوان is a prepositional phrase “in/on the book of the Indians”; note the genitive relationship هندوان (Indians) follows دفتر without an ezâfe, a poetic/genitive phrasing common in classical Persian.
segments.s658-g20: همی + بنگریدم forms a classical continuous/past viewing meaning “I was looking” or simple past “I looked,” where همی marks ongoing action in older Persian. به روشن‌روان is a manner phrase introduced by به, here meaning “with / in (a) bright/clear soul,” describing the attitude or spirit in which the speaker looked.
segments.s658-g21: چنین بد نبشته is a literary formula meaning “thus is it written” (نبشته, a written/past participle, with چنین introducing the content); که begins the subordinate clause “that …”. بر کوه هند is the locative phrase “on Mount Hind/India,” with بر as the preposition and کوه هند the place.
segments.s658-g22: گیاییست is a poetic contraction of گیاهی است (is plant‑like / vegetal) serving as the predicate; چینی is the nominal subject “a Chinese (thing/creature).” چو introduces a simile “as/like,” and رومی پرند is an elliptic simile element—رومی “Roman/Byzantine” qualifying پرند “bird(s)”—so the clause says the Chinese thing is plant‑like and, by comparison, like a Roman bird (i.e. it flies like one). Note the inverted poetic order (predicate before subject).
segments.s658-g23: که introduces a subordinate clause “that …”; آن را is the object marker + demonstrative “that (thing)”; چو here means “when.” گرد آورد (literally “bring around”) is a verbal compound meaning “gathers together,” and رهنمای is the agent ‘the guide’ placed after the verb in this poetic order—so: “that when the guide gathers that together ….”
segments.s658-g24: بیامیزد is a third‑person present/subjunctive poetic form meaning “he mixes (it) in / blends it”; و links the verbs; دانش آرد is “he brings/produces knowledge” (آرد here = ‘brings’), and بجای is an adverbial phrase meaning “in its place / instead” — so the clause means the guide mixes it and in return brings knowledge.
segments.s658-g153: ولیکن (conjunction) = 'but/yet' opens a contrast. بخوانی is the second‑person singular subjunctive/jussive form of خواندن with the prefix ب‑ (used here as a polite command/permission: 'you should/read'); مگر پیش ما is a limiting adverbial phrase: مگر here functions like 'except/only' and پیش ما = 'in our presence' (پیش as 'in the presence of', ما = 'us'). Word order is conjunction + verb + restriction.
segments.s658-g154: بدان is the fusion of the prefix ب‑ + دان (here functioning as an imperative/jussive 'know it' or 'let it be known'). تا introduces a purposive clause 'so that / in order that'. روان is a noun 'soul/spirit', modified by the adjective بداندیش 'ill‑minded, malicious'; ما after the adjective is a genitive/possessive marker 'of us' (i.e. 'the ill‑minded among us').
segments.s658-g155: نگوید here is a negative imperative/prohibitive or formulaic present (classical usage can mean 'do not say' or 'it is not said'); به (preposition) + دل ('heart') marks the indirect object/recipient ('to the heart'). کان is an archaic/connective particle meaning roughly 'that/which' (classical relative), نبشتست is a perfect/passive verbal form 'is written' (نبشتست = 'is/has been written'), and کس = 'anyone/no one' (here 'no one' in sense of 'nobody has written'). The clause therefore prohibits telling the heart something that nobody has written down.
segments.s658-g156: A string of imperatives: بخوان 'read/recite' (imperative or jussive), و = 'and', ب (prefix) + دان = بدان 'know it' (imperative/jussive), و = 'and', ببین 'see/examine' (imperative). پیش و پس is a fixed pair meaning 'before and after' (examine both preceding and following matters). The verbs are coordinated by و.
segments.s658-g157: بدو is the prepositional/clitic form 'to him/unto him' placed before the verb (common in classical word order). گفت is the past tense 'said'. برزوی is the speaker's name (subject) placed after the verb (inverted order). کای شهریار is a vocative phrase: کای is an archaic vocative 'O' (used with kings), شهریار = 'king'.
segments.s658-g158: ندارم is the first‑person present negative 'I do not have / I have not' (ن‑ + دارم). فزون = 'more' or 'additional' (here in comparative sense). ز is the poetic preposition 'than/from' used in comparisons. آنچ = 'that which / what' (classical fused demonstrative/relative 'what you just said'). گویی here refers to 'you say' (second person verb form); مدار at the end is an admonition 'do not (make/do)' or 'do not ask for'—an imperative/negative sense in this context. The whole clause: 'I have no more than what you say; do not [ask for] more.'
segments.s658-g159: کلیله is a proper name (here the person). بیاورد is a third‑person form with the prefix بی‑ (classical/poetic variant of بیـ/بـ) + آورد 'bring' and can express habitual or modal past ('would bring' / 'brought'). گنجور is a compound/adjectival noun 'treasure‑laden' or 'treasury', and شاه following it identifies whose treasury (the king's). Word order places the agent before the verb and the object phrase after.
segments.s658-g160: همی‌بود is an iterative/past habitual copula 'would be / used to be'. او is the third‑person pronoun 'he'. را is the accusative/definite object marker which here is used in a predicative or emphatic role (classical word order often places را after the subject in equational clauses). نماینده = 'representative, envoy', راه = 'road' (here 'on the road' or 'for journeys'). Together: 'he was his representative/agent on the road' (i.e. served as envoy).
segments.s658-g121: This is a subordinate temporal clause introduced by چو (when). زان combines ز + آن and means “from that/those,” modifying رنج‌ها (hardships). برنیامد پدید is a compound verbal phrase: برنیامد (bar-nâyamad) is the negative past/perfective ‘did not come up/appear,’ and پدید reinforces ‘appear/manifest.’ In classical Persian the verb cluster can follow its modifiers, so the whole clause reads “when from those hardships nothing appeared.”
segments.s658-g122: ببایست is an impersonal/modal predicate meaning ‘it is necessary’ / ‘one must.’ ناچار is an adverbial intensifier ‘necessarily/compelled,’ and دیگر here means ‘other/else.’ شنید is the ordinary verb ‘to hear’ (used impersonally: ‘one must hear/attend to something else’). Word order places the modal first, then the adverbs and the lexical verb.
segments.s658-g123: This is a pair of similes. گیا here is an archaic/poetic particle whose exact sense is not among the listed options (see note); چون = ‘like/as,’ linking the comparison to سخن‌دان (‘an eloquent speaker’) and then the second simile ‘دانش چو کوه’ = ‘knowledge like a mountain.’ The two similes are coordinated by و (and).
segments.s658-g124: که introduces a relative clause ‘whose/that’ qualifying the preceding description. همواره is an adverb ‘always,’ باشد is the present/subjunctive form of بودن used here as a general/habitual copula ‘is/remains,’ مر is the dative/benefactive clitic ‘to me/for me’ (classical order), او را marks the person referred to (here ‘his’/‘him’ with topicalizing را), and شکوه is the noun ‘splendor/renown.’ The clause thus says that his splendor is ever (continually) present.
segments.s658-g125: تن مرده is a nominal phrase ‘a dead body/corpse.’ چون introduces a simile ‘like,’ followed by مرد ‘a man/person.’ بیدانشست is a single adjectival/predicative form (bi-dânish-st) meaning ‘is unlearned/ignorant’ — here the verb/copula is incorporated in the archaic ending -ست, so the clause reads ‘a corpse is like an ignorant man.’
segments.s658-g126: که introduces an explanatory/relative clause: دانا is the subject ‘the wise (one),’ به هر جای = ‘in/at every place’ (به = ‘in/at,’ هر = ‘every,’ جای = ‘place’), با marks manner ‘with,’ and رامشست is a predicative form meaning ‘is peaceful/at peace’ (رامش + -ست). The whole clause: ‘for the wise man, in every place, is at peace.’
segments.s658-g127: This is a general statement: به دانش = ‘by/through knowledge’ (به used in the instrumental/causal sense), بود is the copula ‘was/is’ in classical usage, بی‌گمان is an adverb meaning ‘undoubtedly,’ and زنده مرد = ‘a living man’ (زنده modifying مرد). The clause asserts that knowledge is what makes a man truly alive.
segments.s658-g128: چو introduces a conditional ‘if/when’ (چو ... نباشد = ‘if there is not’). دانش = ‘knowledge,’ نباشد is the negative present subjunctive/indicative ‘is not/there is not.’ ب before گردش here marks direction/manner ‘on/to [a] going about,’ گردش is ‘strolling/going about,’ and مگرد is an older/formal negative directive particle meaning ‘do not turn/go’ (imperative/ prohibitive). The whole sentence advises: ‘If there is no knowledge, do not go about.’
segments.s658-g49: بیامد is a past-tense narrative verb (‘he came’). بر + رای is a prepositional phrase: بر here means ‘upon / with regard to’ and رای is ‘counsel / opinion’, so بیامد بر رای = ‘he came to give his counsel’. نامه is the direct object ‘the letter’. بداد is an archaic past form meaning ‘gave/handed’ (here “handed over the letter”); note the provided sense list did not include the verbal sense, so the indexing below marks that mismatch.
segments.s658-g50: سر here means a ‘matter/secret/subject’ (poetic use) rather than anatomical ‘head’. بارها means ‘many times’ (adverbial) not ‘loads’. پیش او is the prepositional phrase ‘before him / in his presence’. برگشاد is a past form of گشودن with the prefix بر- meaning ‘unfolded / opened up (a matter)’. Together: ‘he opened many matters before him.’
segments.s658-g51: چو is the temporal conjunction ‘when’. برخواند is the past of برخواندن meaning ‘read aloud / recited’; it takes آن نامه as its object. شاه is the subject ‘the king’. رای here functions as ‘counsel/opinion’ and begins the king’s following utterance (it ties into the next clause: the king’s counsel/words).
segments.s658-g52: بدو is the dative phrase ‘to him’. گفت is the past verb ‘said’ (the given sense list for گفت gave dialogue/ discussion rather than the simple past ‘said’, so the indexing below notes that mismatch). کای is the vocative particle ‘O’. مرد پاکیزه رای is a noun phrase: مرد ‘man’ modified by the adjective پاکیزه ‘pure/clean’ and followed by رای ‘of counsel’, i.e. ‘man of pure counsel.’
segments.s658-g53: ز is the poetic preposition ‘from’. کسری is the proper name ‘Kisrā / Khosrow’. م (here representing مرا split across tokens) functions as the first part of the object pronoun ‘me / to me’ (the orthography in the gloss is split; the intended meaning is مرا = ‘to me’). را is the definite/object marker attached in the clause. گنج is ‘treasure’. بخشیده is the past participle ‘given/granted’. نیست negates, so the whole clause means ‘from Khosrow, no treasure has been given to me’ (i.e. ‘Khosrow has given me no treasure’). Because the provided senses do not list the pronoun form, that token is indexed below as unmatched.
segments.s658-g54: همه modifies the coordinated noun phrase that follows: ‘all / the whole’. لشکر ‘army’ and پادشاهی ‘kingship / kingdom’ are joined by و. یکیست is a nominal predicate ‘is one’ (third‑person singular) used to say the two form a single whole: ‘the whole army and kingdom are one.’
segments.s658-g55: The repeated z (ز) is the poetic ‘from’ introducing an enumerated list: ‘from justice (داد), from glory (فر), and from Orund (اورند) — the king.’ داد here is the noun ‘justice’ (not a verb), فر is the technical Old/Iranian term for royal glory, and اورند شاه is a proper-name phrase (‘Orund the king’). The repetition of ز emphasizes each source.
segments.s658-g56: وزان is a fused/poetic form (و + زان) meaning ‘and from that’ or ‘and from it’. روشنی modifies بخت: روشنی بخت = ‘the brightness/light of fortune’ or ‘bright fortune’. و آن دستگاه continues the enumeration: و = ‘and’, آن = ‘that’, دستگاه = ‘court/establishment’. Together the phrase links the prior list to ‘that bright fortune and that court.’
segments.s658-g73: پزشکان فرزانه را is a noun phrase marked by the definite/object marker را: 'the wise physicians' as the people summoned. خواند is a past‑tense, 3rd‑person singular verb 'he called/summoned'. رای is a noun 'counsel/opinion' that here complements خواند (i.e. 'called for counsel'). Word order is object (physicians) + verb + complement (counsel), a common classical Persian arrangement where را explicitly flags the summoned party.
segments.s658-g74: کسی کو functions as 'someone who' with کو used as a relative/archaic particle ('who/that'); بدانش means 'in/with that knowledge' (به + دانش compressed), specifying the domain; بدی here does not match the provided modern senses and is best read as an archaic/contracted verbal form meaning 'might be / could be' or a copula-like element; رهنمای is a predicate noun 'a guide/adviser'. Together the clause says 'someone who could be a guide in that knowledge.'
segments.s658-g75: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when'. برزوی is the proper name 'Barzū' in the nominative position. بنهاد is a past tense verb 'he placed/put' (here 'he set'). سر is the direct object 'head'. سوی کوه is a directional phrase 'toward the mountain'. The word order places the temporal clause first, then the subject and verb and the direction phrase.
segments.s658-g76: برفتند is 3rd‑person plural past 'they went/went off'. با او is a prepositional phrase 'with him'. پزشکان گروه is a noun phrase 'a group of physicians' (گروه postmodifies/qualifies پزشکان). The clause means 'a group of physicians went with him.'
segments.s658-g77: پیاده is an adverb/adjective 'on foot'. همه کوهساران is the subject phrase 'all the mountaineers/mountain folk'. بپای here is archaic/poetic and in this context functions adverbially (roughly 'by foot' or as part of the motion expression); the exact supplied senses don't match this usage, so it should be read as a locomotion adverb tied to the verb in the next segment.
segments.s658-g78: بپیمود is a past tense verb 'he/they traversed, accomplished (the journey)'. با introduces an instrument/means 'with'. دانشی is the oblique form 'with (the) knowledge' and رهنمای specifies whose knowledge 'of the guide' (or 'with the guide's guidance'). Together: 'they traversed with the guide's knowledge/under the guide's direction.'
segments.s658-g79: گیاها is the plural noun 'plants/herbs' (collective). ز ... و ز ... is the poetic prepositional pattern 'from the ... and from the ...' (here 'from the dry and from the moist/fresh'). برگزید is the past tense verb 'he chose/selected'. The clause means 'He selected plants from the dry and the moist.'
segments.s658-g80: ز is the preposition 'from'. پژمرده 'withered' is an adjective; و 'and' links the two coordinates; آنچ is the relative/indefinite 'that which / what'; رخشنده is an adjective 'radiant/bright-faced'. دید is the past tense verb 'he saw'. The clause reads 'from the withered and that which was radiant he saw' — i.e. 'he observed the withered ones and those that were radiant.'
segments.s658-g209: دل بدسگالان is a noun phrase meaning “the hearts of the bad‑omened/ill‑willed”; Persian often places the head noun (دل) before its modifier (بدسگالان) without an explicit ezāfe in poetry. شود is third‑person singular (literary) present/subjunctive of شدن meaning “become.” تار و تنگ are two coordinate predicates (joined by و) describing the state the hearts take on; تار here is a poetic adjective meaning “dim/gloomy” (not matched to the provided senses), and تنگ means “narrow/contracted.”
segments.s658-g210: بماند is third‑person singular present/subjunctive of ماندن “remain/last” (here expressing that the beloved’s face will continue to be so). رخ دوست places the noun رخ “face/appearance” modified by دوست “beloved/favourite”; the verb precedes its noun in this poetic inversion. با introduces the accompanying/constitutive qualities “with,” and آب و رنگ is a fixed poetic collocation meaning moisture/tears and colour/complexion (آب = literal water/tears, رنگ = colour/complexion).
segments.s658-g211: یکی آرزو is the direct object phrase “one wish / one thing I ask.” خواهم is first‑person singular present of خواستن and here means “I will want/ask” (literary present used to state a desire). از شهریار marks the source/recipient of the request (“of/from the king”). Word order (object before verb, prepositional phrase at the end) is normal in poetic diction.
segments.s658-g212: که introduces a subordinate clause that gives the content of the wish (“that …”). ماند is third‑person singular present/subjunctive of ماندن and here expresses the desired result (“may/should remain” or simply “remain” as the content of the wish). ز is the poetic form of از “from/of,” so ز من = “from me” (i.e. a memorial of me). در جهان gives the location “in the world,” and یادگار is the thing that remains (the memorial), placed at the clause end as the predicate noun.
segments.s658-g213: چو introduces a temporal clause “when.” بنویسد is third‑person singular present/subjunctive of نوشتن (بنویسد = “he writes/shall write/when he writes”) — in classical/poetic Persian this form can mark a future or contingent action. این نامه is the direct object “this letter.” The proper name بوزرجمهر (Bozorgmehr) is the subject, placed after the object by poetic inversion.
segments.s658-g214: گشاید is third‑person singular present/subjunctive of گشودن (here used figuratively as “open/relieve”). برین is the prepositional phrase بر + این (poetic fusion) meaning “upon/over this.” رنج means “suffering/distress,” and برزوی چهر is a genitive phrase where برزوی identifies Barzū (برزوی = “of/for Barzū”) and چهر = “face/appearance” — together “the distress upon Barzū’s face.” The clause thus states that the action will remove/relieve that distress.
segments.s658-g215: نخستین در functions as the grammatical subject “the foremost/first gate” (نخستین = first; در here = door/gate). از من indicates origin/possession (“of me” / “from me”), کند is third‑person singular present of کردن “makes/does” used to mean “cause/make,” and یادگار is the object “a memorial/memento.” The clause therefore says that the first gate will be made/appointed as a memorial of the speaker.
segments.s658-g216: به فرمان is an instrumental/agentive prepositional phrase meaning “by the command/order.” پیروزگر شهریار is a noun phrase meaning “the victorious/triumphant king” with پیروزگر (triumphant/victor) qualifying شهریار (king). This entire phrase modifies کند, giving the authorization/agency for making the memorial.
