segments.s658-g105: چنین (an adverb) precedes the verb to mark manner: 'thus/so'. گفت is the past verb 'said' placed before the subject ברزوی in the terse, verb‑first style common in epic/persuasive lines. با هندوان indicates the audience ('to/with the Indians') with با used here to mean addressing or being among that group.
segments.s658-g106: که‌ای is a vocative formula here (classical/poetic 'که ای' ≈ 'O') calling out to the addressees. نامداران is a plural adjective/noun 'renowned ones' modifying روشن‌روان 'bright‑souled' (a compound epithet); the two together form the vocative phrase.
segments.s658-g107: برین رنجها is a demonstrative phrase meaning 'these hardships' (بر + ین forming a classical/poetic demonstrative + رنجها 'sufferings/hardships'). بر فزونی کنید is an imperative construction: بر + فزونی ('upon/increase') plus کنید (2nd‑person plural polite imperative 'do/make') — together 'make (them) increase' i.e. 'add to them'.
segments.s658-g108: مرا سوی او رهنمونی کنید is another imperative clause: مرا ('me' in the accusative) is the direct object, سوی او ('toward him') gives the direction, رهنمونی ('guidance') is the noun meaning 'showing the way', and کنید is the 2nd‑person plural imperative 'provide/do'. The whole = 'show/guide me to him.'
segments.s658-g109: مگر introduces an exception/conditional 'unless' here. کان is an archaic/classical particle (here emphatic/pointing to 'that one'), followed by سخنگوی دانای پیر 'the old wise spokesman' (sخنگوی = 'spokesman/speaker', دانای = 'wise', پیر = 'old/elder'). The phrase sets up the conditional subject.
segments.s658-g110: بدین کار = 'in/to this matter' (ب = classical/poetic form of به 'to/in', دین here is part of that fused form), باشد is a third‑person subjunctive/present form 'may be / be able to', and مرا دستگیر is the predicate 'a helper to me' (مرا = 'for me', دستگیر used adjectivally 'helpful, succoring'). Together: '...unless that old wise spokesman is (becomes) helpful to me in this matter.'
segments.s658-g111: ببردند is a past tense verb 'they took/brought' (3rd person plural). برزوی را is the direct object with the accusative marker را, and نزد اوی is the prepositional/directional phrase 'to his presence' (نزد + اوی, an older form of او). Word order (verb – object – prepositional phrase) follows poetic narrative style.
segments.s658-g112: پراندیشه is an adjective 'pensive/thoughtful' modifying the following nouns. دل سر (heart, head) is a paired expression; سر پر از = 'head full of' uses پر ('full [of]') + از ('of') and گفت و گوی is the genitive/compound 'of talk/discussion' (گفت و گو). The clause describes his inward state: thoughtful, his heart and head full of speech/concern.
segments.s658-g129: چو introduces a temporal/conditional clause here ('when'); مردم is a collective noun 'people' treated as a single unit; ز + دانایی marks source/reason 'from wisdom'; آید is a present/ephemeral-form of آمد used here in the sense 'comes/turns' and functions as the verbal nucleus of the clause; ستوه is a predicate adjective 'weary' placed after the verb, so آید ستوه = 'becomes weary'.
segments.s658-g130: گیا is a noun 'plant/vegetation' used literally here; چون introduces a simile 'like/as'; کلیله‌ست is a predicative clause ('is کلیله' or 'is whole/entire' — an older/poetic usage where کلیله functions as an evaluative predicate); و links the two similes; دانش چو کوه places دانش ('knowledge') before the simile phrase 'چو کوه' ('like a mountain'), so the whole segment gives two parallel similes ('the plant is X, and knowledge is like a mountain').
segments.s658-g131: کتابی is an indefinite noun ('a book'); به دانش is a directional/benefactive phrase 'toward knowledge' (literally 'to/for knowledge'); نماینده functions as a participial/adjectival form 'showing, indicating' (i.e. 'that shows'); راه is the noun 'way/method' that نماینده governs—together: 'a book that shows the way to knowledge.'
segments.s658-g132: بیابی is a present/subjunctive/optative-form of یافتن here rendered as 'you find' or 'you will find' (result clause); چو introduces a simile 'as/like'; جویی is an agent-noun 'a seeker' and تو is the explicit pronoun 'you' placed after it for emphasis ('like a seeker, you'); از marks source 'from'; گنج شاه is a genitive phrase 'the king's treasure' (source of what you find).
segments.s658-g133: چو is the temporal 'when'; بشنید is simple past 'heard'; برزوی is the proper name 'Barzuy' (the person affected); ز here is the abbreviated poetic form of 'از' meaning 'of/from' and combines with what was heard; و connects the clauses; شاد شد is a simple past predicate 'became glad' (شاد = adjective, شد = 'became').
segments.s658-g134: همه رنج is the noun phrase 'all suffering'; بر چشم او is a prepositional phrase 'upon his eye(s)' (i.e. before his eyes); باد شد is a classical/poetic construction where باد is an optative particle ('may it be' / a wish) combined with شد ('became') — literally a wish-like formula often translated 'may ... be' or rendered idiomatically depending on context. The whole line thus expresses a resulting blessing/wish about suffering relative to him.
segments.s658-g135: بر او آفرین کرد is literally 'upon him he did praise' — بر او marks the indirect object 'to him' and آفرین ('acclamation/praise') is the thing done; کرد is the past main verb 'did/said'; و شد نزد شاه links the next action — شد here functions like 'came/arrived' (classical use) and نزد شاه is a locative 'in the presence of the king' (نزد = 'before/in the presence of').
segments.s658-g136: به کردار آتش is an adverbial phrase 'in the manner/character of fire' (به + کردار = 'in the way/behaviour of'); آتش is metaphorical ('fire' = speed, ardor); بپیمود is a past form (prefixed ب‑ + پیمود, 'traversed/covered') and is the verb 'he traversed/covered'; راه is the direct object 'the road/way'. Word order is classical: adverbial phrase + verb + object.
segments.s658-g233: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'. مامون is the subject (the caliph Ma'mun) and روشن‌روان is an epithet attached to him. تازه کرد is a verb phrase where تازه ('new/fresh') is what was made and کرد is the past tense 'made/caused'.
segments.s658-g234: خور روز is a poetic juxtaposition meaning 'sun and day' or 'daylight'; بر + دیگر اندازه is a prepositional phrase 'to/into another measure' (بر here 'to/toward'), and کرد is the past verb 'made/changed' governing the whole phrase.
segments.s658-g235: This clause has an implied subject (continuing reference to Ma'mun). دل موبدان داشت literally 'had the hearts of the mobeds' (i.e. their loyalty), and رای کیان is a parallel noun phrase 'the counsel/opinion of the kings' that is also object of داشت.
segments.s658-g236: ببسته is a past participle/verb 'bound/tied' (the direct object is omitted and refers back to 'دل' and 'رای'); به هر دانشی is the beneficiary/goal 'to every kind of knowledge' (به + هر + دانشی); بر میان functions adverbially 'among/in the midst'.
segments.s658-g237: کلیله is the work's title (object). به تازی شد is a passive construction meaning 'became/in Arabic' (here: 'was rendered into Arabic'); از پهلوی marks the source language 'from Pahlavi'.
segments.s658-g238: بدین سان is a fixed phrase 'in this manner' (ب as the prefix + سان 'manner'); که introduces a relative/subordinate clause; اکنون 'now' and همی‌بشنوی is the archaic present 2nd-person singular 'you hear' (همی- marks continuous/habitual present).
segments.s658-g239: بتازی here functions as a contracted/inseparable form meaning 'in Arabic' (poetic compression of به تازی); همی‌بود is an imperfect/continuous 'continued to be'; تا + گاه + نصر = 'until the time of Nasr' (تا = 'until', گاه = 'time', نصر = proper name).
segments.s658-g240: بدانگه 'at that time/thereupon' introduces the temporal clause; که introduces the clause 'when'; شد is the past 'became'; در جهان is the locative 'in the world'; شاه نصر is the nominal 'King Nasr' (name following title).
segments.s658-g41: ببر is the imperative of بردن (’take/bring’), placed at the start as a command. هرچ (= هر چه, “whatever”) is the direct object of that imperative; باید here means ‘necessary/needed’ and qualifies هرچ (i.e. ‘whatever is needed’). به نزدیک رای is a prepositional phrase ‘to/near the council/assembly’ (به + نزدیک + رای), placed after the object to indicate the destination/setting.
segments.s658-g42: کزو is a fused relative/compound preposition meaning ‘from whom / from which’ (کَ + ز + او > کِزو) and introduces the source/agent. بایدت is باید with the enclitic object/beneficiary ت (’for you’), functioning as ‘it is necessary (for you)’; note the synthetic order بایدت rather than separate pronoun. بی‌گمان is an adverb ‘undoubtedly’ modifying the following noun phrase. رهنمای is a nominal predicate ‘(a) guide’ — Persian poetry often omits the copula, so the sense is ‘from whom, without doubt, (you will have/need) a guide.’
segments.s658-g43: در گنج is a fronted prepositional phrase ‘in/inside the treasury’. بگشاد is a poetic past form (from گشودن) meaning ‘opened’ or ‘unlocked’; note the verb follows the locative phrase (poetic inversion). نوشین‌روان is the agent/subject placed after the verb, a common poetic word-order inversion.
segments.s658-g44: ز چیزی که is ‘from/some of those things which…’ — ز introduces a partitive/source, چیزی is the indefinite ‘something/things’, and که is the relative ‘that/which’. بد درخور is an adjective phrase modifying چیزی; in classical/poetic Persian بد can function as an intensifier (here understood as ‘most/very’) or literally ‘bad/ill’ depending on context; the phrase درخور خسروان means ‘fit/appropriate for kings’. The copula is omitted, so the clause describes the kind of things taken from the treasury.
segments.s658-g45: This is a simple coordinate list introduced by the preposition ز (‘from/of’), followed by a series of nouns linked by و (‘and’). The nouns (دینار، دیبا، خز، حریر) are direct objects/items removed from the treasury; Persian often strings items with repeated و for rhythm in poetry.
segments.s658-g46: A continuation of the inventory using repeated ز to mark additional groups. مهر here is an item-word whose exact nuance is context-dependent (jewel/amulet/medal/insignia); افسر is ‘helmet’ (or head‑gear); مشک و عبیر are fragrances (musk and perfume). The repeated ز emphasizes items taken from the same source (the treasury).
segments.s658-g47: شتروار is an adjective/compound meaning ‘in a camel‑train / in long file’ modifying the numeral phrase; سیصد (three hundred) is implicit in the line’s word-order before the participial/adjectival بیاراست. بیاراست is a past or adjectival form meaning ‘adorned/arranged/prepared’. شاه at the end names the agent (the king) — poetic order places the agent after the descriptive phrase.
segments.s658-g48: فرستاده is the subject ‘the messenger’ (one sent by the king). برداشت is the past verb here meaning ‘took up, loaded, removed’ (i.e. took the goods). آمد به راه is a compound-like sequence meaning ‘came on the road’ or idiomatically ‘set out on the road / set off’ — آمد (came) + به راه (‘onto the road’), describing departure.
segments.s658-g137: بیامد is the past/ narrative verb 'came' (3rd sg.) placed at the start as in literary Persian. نیایش‌کنان is a participial/ adverbial phrase ('the ones who pray' / 'praying') modifying the action; پیش رای is a prepositional phrase 'before/in the presence of the council' (پیش = before, رای = counsel/opinion).
segments.s658-g138: که introduces reported speech/ content ('saying that'). تا here has a purposive/conditional sense ('so that' / 'if'). جای باشد is a subjunctive/optative clause 'that there be a place' (باشد = 'may be' / 'is' in literary register). تو is a vocative/address 'you'. بادی is an archaic/poetic form used here as a modality marker meaning roughly 'ought to / should / may be' (not the literal 'windy'); به جای is the prepositional phrase 'in a place' or 'in its proper place'.
segments.s658-g139: کتابیست is the fused literary form of کتاب است 'it is a book' (copula attached to the noun). ای شاه is the vocative 'O king'. گسترده کام is a postpositive adjectival phrase 'of wide scope/ambition' (گسترده = extensive, کام = desire/scope) modifying 'book'.
segments.s658-g140: که introduces a relative clause ('which'). آن را is the object pronoun with the object marker را ('that/it' as direct object). به هندی specifies language/registration 'in the Indian language' (به ... هندی = 'in Indian'). کلیله‌ست is the fused form کلیله است 'it is Kalīla' (copula attached) and نام at the end functions as the predicate 'the name' — together 'is named Kalīla.'
segments.s658-g141: به مهرست is a prepositional phrase meaning 'into the catalog/register' or 'by means of the catalogue' (مهرست here = index/catalog). تا introduces purpose 'so that / in order that'. درج is the noun 'entry/inscription' and در گنج شاه specifies the goal 'into the king's treasury' (در = into/in, گنج شاه = king's treasure/treasury).
segments.s658-g142: به رای و به دانش are two parallel prepositional phrases 'for counsel and for learning' (به = 'for/towards', رای = 'counsel/plan', دانش = 'knowledge'). نماینده راه is a nominal phrase 'indicator/representative of the way' i.e. 'a guide'; نماینده is the head noun and راه ('way') is its complement. Together this qualifies the book as a guide for counsel and learning.
segments.s658-g143: به گنجور is the dative phrase 'to the treasurer / to the treasury-keeper' (گنجور = treasury-house/treasurer). فرمان دهد is 'gives an order' / 'commanded' (فرمان = command, دهد = gives — literary present used narratively). تا introduces the aim 'so that', and ز گنج = 'from the treasury' (ز = from). Together: 'he ordered the treasurer so that (he) take it from the treasury.'
segments.s658-g144: سپارد is the transitive verb 'to entrust / to hand over' (3rd sg.). به introduces the indirect object; من‌گر in the manuscript is a fused sequence (من + گر) — here intended as 'to me' plus an archaic connective meaning 'for / since', so the phrase functions 'and hand it to me; for he does not...' (گر often works as 'for' or 'that' in older Persian). ندارد is 'he does not have / he does not feel', and به رنج 'as a hardship' (رنج = hardship). The whole means 'he will hand it to me, for he does not regard it as a burden.'
segments.s658-g9: This is a noun phrase in apposition: ابر (noun) + modifier هر دری (quantifier + geographic/area noun) + اسم وصفی نامور (adjective 'renowned') + مهتری (an appositive noun meaning 'preeminent one' or 'chief'). Word order is Noun + (modifier) + adjectives/appositive; the whole phrase functions as an epithet describing the person. The phrase is poetically condensed and metaphorical (ابر 'cloud' used as an epithet for a great/towering figure).
segments.s658-g10: An interrogative clause: کجا 'where' at the front, followed by object phrase هر سری را ('every head' with را as the definite/object marker), then بدی which here is an archaic/poetic past form meaning 'you put/gave' (second-person past of دادن) and افسری 'a diadem/helmet' as the direct object. The clause order is Interrogative + object + verb + object-noun; بدی is a poetic verb form not listed among the provided noun senses.
segments.s658-g11: A series of predicates followed by the copula بود: three nouns in apposition (پزشک 'physician', سراینده 'composer/poet', برزوی the proper name). The copula بود in past tense links these predicates to the subject (implied or stated elsewhere); the word order lists roles/titles and then بود ('was').
segments.s658-g12: A resultative/adjectival phrase + predicate: به نیرو رسیده is a participial/result phrase literally 'having reached strength' (به + noun + past participle رسیده) modifying or functioning as predicate, followed by سخنگوی بود 'he was a speaker' (speakership as a role). بود is again the past copula. The structure expresses attainment (رسیده) and then identifies the role (سخنگوی).
segments.s658-g13: Prepositional/partitive phrase zِ + noun with quantifier: ز 'from' + هر دانشی 'every kind of knowledge' (هر quantifies دانشی) + داشتی (past form of داشتن, here 'you had') + بهره‌ای 'a share/portion' (indefinite). Word order is Preposition + quantified noun + verb + indefinite object; داشتی is the past-tense possession verb in poetic register.
segments.s658-g14: Prepositional locative/comparative phrase: به هر بهره‌ای 'in/for every portion' (به introducing the domain) + در جهان 'in the world' (در + noun) + شهره‌ای predicate meaning 'you were famed' or 'famous'. شهره‌ای here is adjectival/predicative, and the clause states renown distributed across each portion/field.
segments.s658-g15: A subordinating result/temporal clause: چنان بد که 'so (it) was that' uses چنان + adjective + که to introduce the ensuing event; روزی is a temporal adverb 'one day/once' (poetic), به هنگام بار 'at the time of rain' (به هنگام + noun). The clause sets up the time and circumstance for the following verb. Note: روزی here is temporal/occasional.
segments.s658-g16: A simple past verb clause: بیامد (past third-person singular) 'came/arrived/fell' + preposition بر 'upon/on' + noun phrase نامور شهریار 'the renowned king' (adjective before noun). The verb is intransitive here and the preposition introduces the affected person (the king).

sentences.s658-s89: Two precious pendants and two earrings, and one jewel‑filled, kingly necklace.
